title: [LLVM][PhaseOrdering] Run CSE after InstCombine has cleaned the result of vectorisation.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @gep_index_cse(ptr %base, i32 %idx) {
entry:
  %idx.ext1 = sext i32 %idx to i64
  %gep1 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx.ext1
  %val1 = load i32, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %idx.ext2 = sext i32 %idx to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx.ext2
  %val2 = load i32, ptr %gep2, align 4
  %add = add i32 %val1, %val2
  %idx.ext3 = sext i32 %idx to i64
  %gep3 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx.ext3
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep3, align 4
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define void @gep_index_cse(ptr %base, i32 %idx) {
entry:
  %idx.ext = sext i32 %idx to i64
  %gep = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx.ext
  %val1 = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %val2 = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  %add = add i32 %val1, %val2
  store i32 %add, ptr %gep, align 4
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Focuses on GEP pointer arithmetic CSE rather than boolean operations from if-conversion. Tests canonicalization of index extensions creating identical GEP computations, which is outside the summary's focus on vectorized loop boolean patterns.

define void @gep_index_cse(ptr %base, i32 %idx) {
entry:

  %val1 = load i32, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %idx.ext2 = sext i32 %idx to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx.ext2
  %val2 = load i32, ptr %gep2, align 4
  %add = add i32 %val1, %val2
  %idx.ext3 = sext i32 %idx to i64
  %gep3 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx.ext3

; Function Attrs: mustprogress norecurse nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)

  %gep1 = getelementptr i32, ptr %base, i64 %idx.ext1

define void @gep_index_cse(ptr %base, i32 %idx) {
entry:

  %val1 = load i32, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %idx.ext2 = sext i32 %idx to i64
  %gep2 = gep ptr %base, 4 x i64 %idx.ext2
  %val2 = load i32, ptr %gep2, align 4

  %idx.ext3 = sext i32 %idx to i64

  store i32 %add, ptr %gep3, align 4

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @bitwiseCanonicalize_cse(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %and1 = and i32 %x, 255
  %and2 = and i32 %x, 255
  %or1 = or i32 %and1, %y
  %or2 = or i32 %and2, %y
  %mul1 = mul i32 %or1, 3
  %mul2 = mul i32 %or2, 3
  %add = add i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i32 %add
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @bitwiseCanonicalize_cse(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %and = and i32 %x, 255
  %or = or i32 %and, %y
  %mul = mul i32 %or, 3
  %add = add i32 %mul, %mul
  ret i32 %add
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests bitwise operation canonicalization (AND/OR) creating CSE opportunities, not covered by existing tests which focus on arithmetic (add/sub/mul) and floating-point operations. Outside the boolean if-conversion vectorization scope.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @select_chain_cse(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %sel1 = select i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %sel2 = select i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %add1 = add i32 %sel1, %c
  %add2 = add i32 %sel2, %c
  %mul1 = mul i32 %add1, 2
  %mul2 = mul i32 %add2, 2
  %result = add i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @select_chain_cse(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b
  %add = add i32 %sel, %c
  %mul = mul i32 %add, 2
  %result = add i32 %mul, %mul
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests select instruction CSE patterns rather than branch-based if-conversion. While related to control flow, this focuses on select instruction canonicalization outside the loop vectorization context described in the summary.
